name: py-dom-xpath-six
documentation:
py-dom-xpath is a pure Python implementation of XPath 1.0. It
supports almost all XPath 1.0, with the main exception being the
namespace axis. It operates on DOM 2.0 nodes, and works well with
xml.dom.minidom, but also with other xml.dom implementations.

py-dom-xpath-six is a version of py-dom-xpath that is compatible with Python
3.6 and later. It is a fork of py-dom-xpath-redux, which in turn is a fork of
the original py-dom-xpath.

Changes in 0.2.4:
- Fix compatibility issue with Python 3
- Got rid of Python 2 support
- Minor changes to update to newer standards for pypi, etc.

Changes in 0.2.3:
- Optimized sorting of nodesets in document order: do only at end of expression.

Here is the original README file:

py-dom-xpath is a pure Python implementation of XPath 1.0. It
supports almost all XPath 1.0, with the main exception being the
namespace axis. It operates on DOM 2.0 nodes, and works well with
xml.dom.minidom.

py-dom-xpath requires Python 2.5 or greater.

py-dom-xpath uses the Yapps 2 parser generator by Amit J. Patel.
Portions of of Yapps 2 are included in this distribution, to avoid
introducing external dependencies.

py-dom-xpath was developed at Nominum. Nominum has graciously
permitted the author to release it under the MIT license.

For further details, visit the project home page at:

    http://code.google.com/p/py-dom-xpath/

Get the i'th token, and if i is one past the end, then scan
for another token; restrict is a list of tokens that
are allowed, or 0 for any token.

Returns the token type for lookahead; if there are any args

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathError
documentation: Base exception class used for all XPath exceptions.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathNotImplementedError
documentation:
Raised when an XPath expression contains a feature of XPath which
has not been implemented.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathParseError
documentation: Raised when an XPath expression could not be parsed.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathTypeError
documentation:
Raised when an XPath expression is found to contain a type error.
For example, the expression "string()/node()" contains a type error
because the "string()" function does not return a node-set.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathUnknownFunctionError
documentation:
Raised when an XPath expression contains a function that has no
binding in the expression context.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathUnknownPrefixError
documentation:
Raised when an XPath expression contains a QName with a namespace
prefix that has no corresponding namespace declaration in the expression
context.

classes.name: xpath.exceptions.XPathUnknownVariableError
documentation:
Raised when an XPath expression contains a variable that has no
binding in the expression context.

functions.name: xpath.api
documentation:
Decorator for functions and methods that are part of the external
module API and that can throw XPathError exceptions.

The call stack for these exceptions can be very large, and not very
interesting to the user.  This decorator rethrows XPathErrors to
trim the stack.

modules.name: yapps2.yapps2
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Yapps 2.0 - yet another python parser system
Amit J Patel, January 1999
See http://theory.stanford.edu/~amitp/Yapps/ for documentation and updates
v2.0.1 changes (October 2001):
* The exceptions inherit the standard Exception class (thanks Rich Salz)
* The scanner can use either a different set of regular expressions
per instance, or allows the subclass to define class fields with
the patterns.  This improves performance when many Scanner objects
are being created, because the regular expressions don't have to
be recompiled each time. (thanks Amaury Forgeot d'Arc)
v2.0.2 changes (April 2002)
* Bug fix: generating the 'else' clause when the comment was too
long.  v2.0.1 was missing a newline.  (thanks Steven Engelhardt)
v2.0.3 changes (August 2002)
* Bug fix: inline tokens using the r"" syntax.
v.2.0.4 changes (July 2003)
* Style change: Replaced `expr` with repr(expr)
* Style change: Changed (b >= a and b < c) into (a <= b < c)
* Bug fix: identifiers in grammar rules that had digits in them were
not accessible in the {{python code}} section
* Bug fix: made the SyntaxError exception class call
Exception.__init__ (thanks Alex Verstak)
* Style change: replaced raise "string exception" with raise
ClassException(...) (thanks Alex Verstak)

functions.name: xpath.expr._round
documentation: Workaround for Python 2 and 3 differences

functions.name: xpath.expr.string_value
documentation: Compute the string-value of a node.
Data model functions.

functions.name: xpath.expr.document_order
documentation:
Compute a document order value for the node.

cmp(document_order(a), document_order(b)) will return -1, 0, or 1 if
a is before, identical to, or after b in the document respectively.

We represent document order as a list of sibling indexes.  That is,
the third child of the document node has an order of [2].  The first
child of that node has an order of [2,0].

Attributes have a sibling index of -1 (coming before all children of
their node) and are further ordered by name--e.g., [2,0,-1,'href'].

functions.name: xpath.expr.nodeset
documentation: Convert a value to a nodeset.
Type functions, operating on the various XPath types.
Internally, we use the following representations:
nodeset - list of DOM tree nodes in document order
string  - str or unicode
boolean - bool
number  - int or float

functions.name: xpath.expr.nodesetp
documentation: Return true iff 'v' is a node-set.

documentation: Convert a value to a string.

functions.name: xpath.expr.stringp
documentation: Return true iff 'v' is a string.

functions.name: xpath.expr.boolean

functions.name: xpath.expr.number

functions.name: xpath.expr.axisfn
documentation:
Axis function decorator.

An axis function will take a node as an argument and return a sequence
over the nodes along an XPath axis.  Axis functions have two extra
attributes indicating the axis direction and principal node type.

functions.name: xpath.expr.make_axes
documentation: Define functions to walk each of the possible XPath axes.

functions.name: xpath.expr.merge_into_nodeset
documentation:
Place all the nodes from the source node-set into the target
node-set, preserving document order.  Both node-sets must be in
document order to begin with.
If dontsort is True the resulting list isn't sorted, but in stead a True
value is returned to indicate sorting needs to be done later.

classes.name: xpath.expr.Expr

methods.name: xpath.expr.Expr.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate the expression.

The context node, context position, and context size are passed as
arguments.

Returns an XPath value: a nodeset, string, boolean, or number.
Abstract base class for XPath expressions.

classes.name: xpath.expr.Function

methods.name: xpath.expr.Function.function
documentation:
Function decorator.

minargs -- Minimum number of arguments taken by the function.
maxargs -- Maximum number of arguments taken by the function.
implicit -- True for functions which operate on a nodeset consisting
            of the current context node when passed no argument.
            (e.g., string() and number().)
convert -- When non-None, a function used to filter function arguments.
XPath functions are implemented by methods of the Function class.
A method implementing an XPath function is decorated with the function
decorator, and receives the evaluated function arguments as positional
parameters.

documentation: Node Set Functions

methods.name: xpath.expr.Function.f_string
documentation: String Functions

methods.name: xpath.expr.Function.f_boolean
documentation: Boolean functions

methods.name: xpath.expr.Function.f_number
documentation: Number functions
